stories.headline: News live: Trump says Australia ‘didn’t help’ with Iran war; ‘increasing’ cyclone threat for Queensland
stories.summary: US president criticises Australia, Japan and South Korea; chance of Cyclone Maila hitting Queensland at the weekend is rising, BoM says. stories.headline: Trump warns Iran it could be destroyed 'in one night' as deadline nears
stories.summary: Donald Trump has once again escalated threats against Iran, warning the country could be “taken out in one night” as the US deadline for Tehran approaches. Iran has rejected a proposed ceasefire, insisting on a permanent end to the war, while fighting continues to intensify across the region.

stories.headline: Iran rejects ceasefire proposal with US as war of words deepens
stories.summary: Iran rejects a new 45-day ceasefire proposal, while the US is still expected to respond, as US President Donald Trump delays a threatened military strike tied to the Strait of Hormuz. Mediators are pushing for talks, but tensions remain high on both sides.

stories.headline: Trump Threatens to Hit Civilian Targets If Iran Doesn’t Open Hormuz
stories.summary: On today’s Big Take podcast: Unpacking President Trump’s mixed rhetoric on Iran, the status of ceasefire talks and why he wants to collect tolls for the strait.

stories.headline: Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war
stories.summary: US president says Washington, as the 'winner' of the war, has a 'concept' for charging a toll in the strategic waterway.

stories.headline: Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive
stories.summary: Several members of US Congress question president's fitness for office after his expletive-laden Easter Sunday message.

stories.headline: Trump warns Hormuz deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war
stories.summary: US president says proposal not enough to avert threat to infrastructure should Iran fail to open Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
stories.summary: The Israeli strikes reportedly targeted Hamas security personnel after they clashed with an Israeli-backed militia near Maghazi camp.

stories.headline: Israeli army fire on WHO vehicle in southern Gaza kills one, medics report
stories.summary: WHO driver Majdi Aslan was killed and a WHO doctor wounded, along with several other Palestinians.

stories.headline: How Asia became ground zero for the oil crisis
stories.summary: As the Iran war disrupts oil flows, fuel shortages hit Asia first. Is this the start of a wider global energy crisis?

stories.headline: Libya’s oil disputes mirror Hormuz crisis, fuel European energy fears
stories.summary: Libyan oil sector proxy wars occur during Hormuz tensions, creating dangerous supply vulnerabilities for global markets.

stories.headline: ASEAN+3 growth may hit 4-year low if Iran war drags on: economist

stories.summary: Drones are reported to have fired missiles into an area near Maghazi refugee camp.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court paves way for dismissal of Steve Bannon conviction
stories.summary: The order allows a lower court to consider dismissing the former Trump strategist's indictment.

stories.headline: UK to host meeting on safe passage through Strait of Hormuz after war

stories.headline: EU warns capitals against turning energy crunch into fiscal crisis

stories.headline: Underwater Mortgages Force China’s Banks to Get More Creative
stories.summary: Lenders are trying to prevent defaults and foreclosures that risk deepening the country’s housing crisis.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Clears the Way for Dismissal of Steve Bannon’s Conviction
stories.summary: Stephen K. Bannon, a former close aide to President Trump, was convicted for failing to comply with a congressional subpoena related to the investigation into the Jan. 6 attack.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Syria to cooperate on security in unexpected military alliance
stories.summary: Ukraine's Zelenskyy arrived in Damascus in a surprise visit on Sunday, for his first official visit to Syria after the fall of the pro-Russian al-Assad regime.

stories.summary: Steve Bannon, an ally of US President Donald Trump, served a four-month prison sentence after his 2022 conviction.

stories.summary: Greek PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis has asked EU prosecutors to swiftly charge MPs over alleged €23m farm subsidy fraud, as resignations and pressure mount.

stories.headline: When Suzuki met Suzuki: why a Tokyo dating agency is matching couples with the same name
stories.summary: Japan’s ban on married couples having different surnames has prompted an event to highlight people’s reluctance to change their nameAt the very least, the three men and three women calming their nerves on a Friday evening at a venue in Tokyo know they have one thing in common.Spaced out across booths, they will soon be placed in pairs and given 15 minutes to get to know one another. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
stories.summary: Several footballers have absconded in the past leaving behind what rights groups call a repressive government.

stories.headline: Why this oil shock is different
stories.summary: Governments and central banks are out of policy ammunition to contain the economic fallout

stories.headline: Sales of used EVs surge in US as petrol prices pass $4 a gallon
stories.summary: Americans are buying second-hand electric vehicles even as the market for new vehicles slumps

stories.headline: Oil Holds Near Highest Since 2022 as Trump Threats Escalate
stories.summary: US oil held near its highest close since June 2022 as President Donald Trump escalated threats to obliterate key Iranian infrastructure if his terms aren’t met before a Tuesday deadline.

stories.headline: One-Dyas Expands Gas Output in Protected North Sea Marine Zone
stories.summary: One-Dyas BV has started production from a second well at its platform in the North Sea, lifting annual output at the site to about 1 billion cubic meters of gas.

stories.headline: Two Sigma Profits From Chaotic March, Beating Multistrat Peers
stories.summary: Two Sigma Investments’ biggest hedge funds generated standout returns during a frantic month for markets, edging out peers even as the firm grapples with executive infighting.

stories.headline: Goldman Says It’s Ready to Pounce as Retail Flees Private Credit
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. says a reliance on stickier, more patient institutional investors, rather than wealthy individuals, helped its $15.7 billion private credit fund narrowly escape the broader exodus plaguing peers this year.

stories.headline: 2026 is World’s Largest Sports Year: Paul Caine
stories.summary: On Location President Paul Caine discusses the economic ripple effects of the FIFA World Cup, why 2026 is set to be a monumental year for sporting events, and the growth of sports related travel. He talks with Romaine Bostick and Isabelle Lee on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Medicare Finalizes 2.48% Rate Hike
stories.summary: The US Medicare program will pay private insurers 2.48% more in 2027, a meaningful improvement over the initial rates the agency proposed in January. Michelle Cortez has more on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Next Gen Bitcoin ETFs, Refiners Outperform Broader Market | ETF IQ 4/6/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg ETF IQ" focuses on the opportunities, risks and current trends tied to the trillions of dollars in the global exchange traded funds industry. Today's guests: State Street Investment Management Global Head of Research Strategist Matt Bartolini and PIMCO Generalist Portfolio Manager Dave Braun. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Is SpaceX Worth $2 Trillion? Key Questions for Musk’s Big IPO
stories.summary: In the seven and a half years since Apple Inc.’s market capitalization broke through the $1 trillion mark, investors have become accustomed to seeing big tech companies command 13-digit valuations in public markets.

stories.headline: US Stocks Gain Despite Trump’s Threat to ‘Take Out’ Iran
stories.summary: US equities posted a fourth straight day of gains even as President Donald Trump said Iran could be “taken out” Tuesday night, while oil prices climbed.

stories.headline: MAK Capital Tells Evotec to List US Unit Worth Over €1 billion
stories.summary: Activist fund MAK Capital, led by Michael A. Kaufman, has urged the leadership of German biotech firm Evotec SE to list its US unit and speed up its cost cutting program after the shares have lost about 85% over the past five years.

stories.headline: Texas Congressman Accused of Pursuing a Second Subordinate With Lewd Texts
stories.summary: A news report linked Representative Tony Gonzales of Texas, who has admitted to an affair with an aide, to another series of sexual texts with a different aide, raising a dilemma for the House G.O.P.

stories.headline: Shots Fired at Indianapolis Councilman’s Home, After Vote Backing Data Center
stories.summary: No one was injured, but the councilman, Ron Gibson, called it “deeply unsettling.”

stories.headline: The California Lake Billed as the ‘Saudi Arabia of Lithium’
stories.summary: Residents of Imperial County, Calif., are in dire need of an economic boost. Experts say the answer lies beneath the Salton Sea, where a lithium trove sits.

stories.headline: Foreign investment in Spain falls by 22% to lowest level since 2021
stories.summary: FDI in Spain fell by 21.8% in 2025 to €30.764 billion, its lowest level since 2021 despite the expected boost from European funds, according to official data.

stories.headline: Pro-Orbán actors accuse Meta of interference in Hungarian elections, despite lack of evidence
stories.summary: Meta has found itself embroiled in Hungary's tense election campaign, after supporters of the ruling Fidesz party claimed their posts are being restricted to favour opposition parties. In reality, there is no weight to the allegations.

stories.headline: Revellers parade giant phalluses at Japan’s fertility festival
stories.summary: Crowds pack Kawasaki for Kanamara Matsuri, Japan’s colourful fertility festival using phallic shrines to celebrate life and challenge sex taboos.

stories.headline: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
stories.summary: It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine.

stories.headline: Spain's huge pork industry seeks salvation from swine fever threat
stories.summary: Countries around the world, including the US, have already stopped imports over the outbreak.

stories.headline: Two protests, two elections: How Nepal's Gen Z succeeded where Bangladesh's stumbled
stories.summary: How did a youth movement triumph in one country but fall so short in another?

stories.summary: Cultural figures sign open letter asking government for clarity on how long landmark collection will remain abroadOne of the world’s most important collections of 20th-century Mexican art, including works by Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera, is set to be exported to Spain under an agreement with Banco Santander, sparking outrage among Mexico’s cultural community.Nearly 400 cultural professionals have signed an open letter calling on the Mexican gover

stories.headline: Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky
stories.summary: Six civilians were killed and 40 others injured as Russia launched hundreds of drones and missiles.

stories.headline: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war

stories.headline: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warningOne person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves.The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an

stories.headline: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
stories.summary: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

stories.headline: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
stories.summary: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

stories.headline: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'

stories.headline: Asia's migrant workers debate if Gulf jobs are worth deadly risk of Iran war
stories.summary: Iran's strikes on Gulf states have been especially hard for migrants who have long supported these economies to lift their families back home from poverty.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gustsAn unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia.
